item.text: I’ll verify the two follow-up commits against the r3 baseline, then run the focused monitoring tests if the repo allows it. I’ll keep this review read-only.

The shell wrapper is failing before commands run (`bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR`). I’m going to retry with a minimal non-login shell invocation; if that still fails, I’ll use the available file-oriented tools where possible and call out anything I could not verify.

Local command execution is consistently blocked, so I’m switching to the GitHub connector for commit and file inspection. I’ll use the supplied SHAs to identify the repo and compare `7a0f18f..22db812`.

- `app/panels/Financials/Dashboard.tsx` — KPI grid + sparkline
- `app/panels/Financials/GreenBar.tsx` — ledger print view
- `app/panels/Financials/Sparkline.tsx` — minimal SVG sparkline (pure Solid, no charting lib)
- `app/panels/Financials/format.ts` — `formatMoney`, `formatLedger`, `formatDelta`, `computeKPIs`, `eraLabel`
- `app/panels/Financials/types.ts` — `FinancialEntry`, `WeeklyPnL`, `EraSlug` interfaces

## Novo arquivo: `frontend/src/constants/terms.ts`

Exporta `TERMS` tipado com `as const` organizado por domínio:

```ts
export const TERMS = {
  financeiro: { fluxoCaixa: "Fluxo de Caixa", contasPagar: "Contas a Pagar", indiceLiquidez: "Índice de Liquidez", ... },
  contabil:   { dre: "Demonstrativo de Resultados (DRE)", receitaBruta: "Receita Bruta", lucroLiquido: "Lucro Líquido", ... },
  graficos:   { legendaEntradas: "Entradas", legendaSaidas: "Saídas", legendaReceitas: "Receitas", ... },
  auth:       { entrarSistema: "Entrar no Sistema", erroLoginFalha: "Falha ao realizar login...", ... },
  // + brand, dashboard, multiempresa, compliance, onboarding, sidebar, landing, ui
} as const;

export type Terms = typeof TERMS;
```

## Aplicado em 11 arquivos (prioridade 1–3)

- **`LandingPage.jsx`** — hero, CTAs, seções, footer, mensagem de erro
- **`Dashboard.jsx`** — labels KPI, badges, títulos de gráficos, legendas Recharts com `name={TERMS…}`, audit panel
- **`Login.jsx`** — labels, placeholders, mensagens de validação Zod, botão submit
- **`Sidebar.jsx`** — todos os itens de menu, grupos de seção, botões, footer compliance
- **6 componentes `Landing/`** (`HeroFinancialChart`, `KPIStatsCards`, `MiniDRECard`, `CashFlowLineChart`, `CostDistributionChart`, `MultiEmpresaTable`) — tooltips, legendas, headers de tabela, labels de KPI

## Correções específicas

- **Legendas de gráfico de barras (Dashboard):** `dataKey="Receitas"` era usado implicitamente pelo Recharts como label de legenda. Renomeadas as chaves para lowercase (`receitas`, `despesas`) e adicionados `name={TERMS.graficos.legendaReceitas/Despesas}` explícitos.
- **Chave duplicada:** `entrarSistema` aparecia duas vezes no bloco `auth` do dicionário — removida a duplicata.
- **Typo de chave:** `alternárEmpresa` (com acento) renomeada para `alternarEmpresa`.

## Escopo intencionalmente fora desta PR

Telas financeiras e contábeis restantes (`Tesouraria`, `ContasPagar`, `ContasReceber`, páginas de Contabilidade) ainda usam strings hardcoded — as chaves necessárias já estão no dicionário, a aplicação fica para etapa seguinte.", "state": null, "user": null, "assignees": null, "labels": null, "milestone": null, "state_reason": null, "comments": null, "created_at": null, "updated_at": null, "closed_at": null, "display_url": "https://github.com/AlfredPennywhorth/sistema-erp/pull/14", "display_title": "feat: Dicionário Oficial de Termos pt-BR — camada central de terminologia no frontend"}]}

issues.body:
## Summary

- **Dashboard view** (default): 4 KPI cards (bankroll, last-week net, runway weeks, top expense category) + 12-week SVG sparkline with no charting library
- **Green-bar view**: classic tractor-feed P&L ledger — alternating green/cream rows, monospace (VT323/Courier), running-balance column, header/footer bands
- **Era-aware formatting**: classic eras (`carnival-catch`, `golden-age`, `territorial`) show `$X.XX`; modern eras use `$1.2K`/`$3.4M` abbreviations. All values in integer cents.

## Files

- `app/panels/Financials/Financials.tsx` — panel shell with view toggle signal
- `app/panels/Financials/Dashboard.tsx` — KPI grid + sparkline
- `app/panels/Financials/GreenBar.tsx` — ledger print view
- `app/panels/Financials/Sparkline.tsx` — minimal SVG sparkline (pure Solid, no charting lib)
- `app/panels/Financials/format.ts` — `formatMoney`, `formatLedger`, `formatDelta`, `computeKPIs`, `eraLabel`
- `app/panels/Financials/types.ts` — `FinancialEntry`, `WeeklyPnL`, `EraSlug` interfaces
- `app/panels/Financials/index.ts` — barrel
- `app/styles/panels/financials.css` — modern dashboard + green-bar paper texture surfaces
- `tests/ui/panels/Financials.test.tsx` — 48 unit tests covering formatting and KPI computation

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test` — 230 tests pass (48 new)
- [x] `pnpm typecheck` — clean
- [x] `pnpm lint` — exit 0, no errors in new files
- [ ] Visual: dashboard KPI cards render with correct era formatting
- [ ] Visual: ledger alternating green/cream rows, monospace alignment
- [ ] Toggle button switches views; `aria-pressed` state tracks correctly
- [ ] Runway shows `null → "Profitable"`, low runway shows `"Critical"` with pulse animation

issues.title: feat: Dicionário Oficial de Termos pt-BR — camada central de terminologia no frontend
issues.body:
Todo texto visível ao usuário estava hardcoded e inconsistente entre telas — mistura de pt-BR com inglês, variação de termos para o mesmo conceito (ex: "Saldo em Caixa" vs "Saldo Total em Conta" sem distinção semântica clara), e labels divergentes em tooltips e legendas de gráficos.

## Novo arquivo: `frontend/src/constants/terms.ts`

Exporta `TERMS` tipado com `as const` organizado por domínio:

```ts
export const TERMS = {
  financeiro: { fluxoCaixa: "Fluxo de Caixa", contasPagar: "Contas a Pagar", indiceLiquidez: "Índice de Liquidez", ... },
  contabil:   { dre: "Demonstrativo de Resultados (DRE)", receitaBruta: "Receita Bruta", lucroLiquido: "Lucro Líquido", ... },
  graficos:   { legendaEntradas: "Entradas", legendaSaidas: "Saídas", legendaReceitas: "Receitas", ... },
  auth:       { entrarSistema: "Entrar no Sistema", erroLoginFalha: "Falha ao realizar login...", ... },
  // + brand, dashboard, multiempresa, compliance, onboarding, sidebar, landing, ui
} as const;

export type Terms = typeof TERMS;
```

## Aplicado em 11 arquivos (prioridade 1–3)

- **`LandingPage.jsx`** — hero, CTAs, seções, footer, mensagem de erro
- **`Dashboard.jsx`** — labels KPI, badges, títulos de gráficos, legendas Recharts com `name={TERMS…}`, audit panel
- **`Login.jsx`** — labels, placeholders, mensagens de validação Zod, botão submit
- **`Sidebar.jsx`** — todos os itens de menu, grupos de seção, botões, footer compliance
- **6 componentes `Landing/`** (`HeroFinancialChart`, `KPIStatsCards`, `MiniDRECard`, `CashFlowLineChart`, `CostDistributionChart`, `MultiEmpresaTable`) — tooltips, legendas, headers de tabela, labels de KPI

## Correções específicas

- **Legendas de gráfico de barras (Dashboard):** `dataKey="Receitas"` era usado implicitamente pelo Recharts como label de legenda. Renomeadas as chaves para lowercase (`receitas`, `despesas`) e adicionados `name={TERMS.graficos.legendaReceitas/Despesas}` explícitos.
- **Chave duplicada:** `entrarSistema` aparecia duas vezes no bloco `auth` do dicionário — removida a duplicata.
- **Typo de chave:** `alternárEmpresa` (com acento) renomeada para `alternarEmpresa`.

## Escopo intencionalmente fora desta PR

Telas financeiras e contábeis restantes (`Tesouraria`, `ContasPagar`, `ContasReceber`, páginas de Contabilidade) ainda usam strings hardcoded — as chaves necessárias já estão no dicionário, a aplicação fica para etapa seguinte.

issues.title: ci: redesign api_contracts.yml with two-phase spec validation and oasdiff enforcement
issues.body:
Currently api.json is not being updated when `make all` is run. Here are the changes to `api_contracts.yml`:

1. Get base branch api.json
2. Run "make openapi" to generate api_gen.json
3. Compare api_gen.json with the PR's api.json, if there is a different, return an error with instructions to run "make openapi" to update api.json.
4. Next, do osadiff to check base branch with api_gen.json to check for breaking changes

* **Chores**
  * Enhanced API contract validation workflow with automated freshness checks and breaking-change detection in pull requests
  * Added acknowledgment mechanism for intentional breaking API changes to streamline review process
  * Improved build toolchain caching and environment configuration for faster CI/CD pipelines

issues.title: Issue #49: Restore swarm runtime baseline
issues.body:
Fixes #49\n\n## What\n- Upstreams the known-good swarm runtime baseline so lane runners can start reliably (lane.json flag compatibility, deterministic lanes, dashboard, keepalive).\n- Includes current travel-safe local-only lane defaults used to keep the swarm operational while nodes are missing.\n\n## Evidence\n- python3 -m py_compile src/orxaq_autonomy/*.py scripts/autonomy_runner.py
bash -n scripts/autonomy_manager.sh scripts/preflight.sh scripts/generate_workspace.sh scripts/open_vscode.sh scripts/install_keepalive.sh\n- python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py'

{"issues": [{"url": "https://github.com/jeremiah-k/mtjk/pull/78", "title": "Refactor BLE interface and add node runtime architecture", "issue_number": 78, "body": "

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Overview

This PR refactors the BLE/node integration by introducing a new node_runtime subsystem and moving many node-related responsibilities (channel management, settings, transport, admin handling, content, setURL transactions, and response processing) into focused runtime classes. It also applies a series of small BLE-interface cleanups and adds extensive unit tests across the new runtimes and related behaviors. The changes preserve public APIs via compatibility shims where needed and aim to improve thread-safety, typing, and test coverage.

## Key changes

### Features
- New node_runtime subsystem that centralizes node-facing logic into many focused runtimes:
  - Node/channel management: _NodeChannelLookupRuntime, _NodeChannelNormalizationRuntime, _NodeChannelPresentationRuntime, _NodeChannelExportRuntime
  - Channel bootstrap and requests: _NodeChannelRequestRuntime
  - Settings and admin commands: _NodeSettingsMessageBuilder, _NodeSettingsRuntime, _NodeSettingsResponseRuntime, _NodeAdminCommandRuntime
  - Content (ringtones/canned messages): _NodeContentCacheStore, _NodeContentResponseRuntime, _NodeAdminContentRuntime
  - Admin transport and session/ACK handling: _NodeAdminSessionRuntime, _NodeAdminTransportRuntime, _NodeChannelWriteRuntime, _NodeDeleteChannelRuntime, _NodeAckNakRuntime, _NodePositionTimeCommandRuntime
  - Response handling: _NodeMetadataResponseRuntime, _NodeChannelResponseRuntime
  - SetURL pipeline: full parser, planners (add-only/replace-all), execution engine, rollback engine, transaction coordinator, and cache manager
  - Shared utilities/constants: meshtastic.node_runtime.shared (limits, helper functions, constants)
- New convenience/compatibility shims (snake_case ↔ camelCase) for some exposed operations to preserve backwards compatibility.
- Many new unit test modules covering all new runtimes, setURL flows, response handling, transport behavior, and related integrations.

### Fixes / Behavioral adjustments
- SetURL execution/rollback behavior refined (detailed planning, ordered writes, LoRa handling, and more conservative rollback when writes were not started).
- Channel write/primary-channel encryption mutation includes cache consistency checks and explicit invalidation when local cache changes during writes.
- Content (ringtone/canned) runtime: robust fragment accumulation, terminal response detection, cache promotion, and write chunking for large canned messages.
- Channel response runtime: retry logic for routing errors with a single retry limit and terminal failure handling for repeated failures.
- Several lifecycle/ownership hooks resolved more robustly via a centralized _resolve_hook helper, reducing repeated getattr/callable checks and handling unconfigured mock callables.

### Refactors / style
- Major architectural refactor: splitting responsibilities into the node_runtime package and many internal runtime classes to improve modularity, concurrency handling, and testability.
- BLE interface files: minor formatting and lint-comment relocations in compatibility_service.py, errors.py, interface.py, runner.py — no semantic changes.
- Added select pylint suppressions (unnecessary-ellipsis, redefined-outer-name) where appropriate.
- Minor logic simplifications (stale-client closure guard adjusted, small predicate condensing, removal of cast wrappers).

### Tests
- Extensive new test coverage added for setURL pipeline (parser, planners, execution, rollback, cache manager, coordinator).
- New test suites for channel export, lookup, normalization, presentation, request, content runtime, response runtime, settings runtime, transport runtime, and lifecycle controller.
- Existing tests updated (test_mesh_interface.py, test_node.py) to reflect refactored internals and snapshot-based channel-write semantics.

## Breaking changes / migration notes

- No public API signature changes were introduced: exported/public function and class signatures remain unchanged, and compatibility aliases were added where camelCase → snake_case differences exist. The refactor is internal and aims to be backward-compatible.
- Consumers relying on internal, undocumented behaviors could observe differences in timing or caching semantics due to stricter snapshotting and cache-consistency checks; if tests or integrations relied on in-place mutation of cached channel objects, update to use the runtime snapshot-based accessors (the new internals favor defensive copies under locks).
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->", "state": null, "user": null, "assignees": null, "labels": null, "milestone": null, "state_reason": null, "comments": null, "created_at": null, "updated_at": null, "closed_at": null, "display_url": "https://github.com/jeremiah-k/mtjk/pull/78", "display_title": "Refactor BLE interface and add node runtime architecture"}, {"url": "https://github.com/jeremiah-k/mtjk/pull/54", "title": "Refactor codebase with constants, sync, and test improvements", "issue_number": 54, "body": "

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Overview

This PR is a coordinated refactor that improves thread-safety, validation, and robustness across the codebase and substantially expands unit test coverage. It centralizes many magic numbers into documented constants, tightens locking and input validation (notably for Node/channel state, BLE flows, TCP/stream writes, OTA transfers, serial handling, and tunnel/IP mapping), and preserves backwards compatibility by adding canonical CamelCase names while keeping snake_case shims. Tests and test tooling were modernized for deterministic timing, better fixtures, and broader concurrency/edge-case coverage.

## Key changes

Features
- New module-level constants for timeouts, sizes and limits (examples: OTA_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, OTA_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES, FILE_HASH_READ_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES, SERVICE_CHARACTERISTIC_RETRY_DELAY, EMPTY_READ_MAX_DELAY, TRANSIENT_READ_MAX_DELAY, WRITE_PROGRESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, MAX_CHANNELS, MAX_SHORT_NAME_LEN, METADATA_STDOUT_COMPAT_WAIT_SECONDS, LOG_DIR_COLLISION_MAX_RETRIES, SERIAL_PORT_PATH_EMPTY_ERROR, UNKNOWN_SNR_QUARTER_DB, WRITE_TIMEOUT_ERROR/WRITE_NO_PROGRESS_ERROR).
- OTA: streaming/chunked transfer, file-hashing helpers (hashBytes/hashHex) and compatibility aliases; socket-timeout and chunk-size constants; progress callback support and parameterized logging.
- BLE: retry pacing for characteristic checks, discovery-factory validation with richer errors, discovery fallback when direct connect fails (including device-not-found detection), notification registration retries for BlueZ "notify acquired" conflicts with graceful fallback to polling, and guarded reconnection semantics.
- TCP / stream: partial-send loop using select, write-progress deadlines, unified STREAM_IO_EXCEPTIONS, and explicit write-timeout/error messages to avoid stalled writes.
- Public compatibility surface: many CamelCase canonical names added with snake_case aliases retained (examples: getActiveVersion/get_active_version, detectWindowsPort/detect_windows_port, analysis/powermon/slog aliases).
- Test tooling: new CLI helpers (runCliWithTimeout/runCliArgvWithTimeout/_quote_shell_path), pytest marker smoke1_destructive, and new test fixtures/helpers (ble_client, notification_manager, build_interface, _managed_tunnel, and others).

Fixes and robustness improvements
- Stronger validation and clearer errors:
  - Host/port parsing rejects empty/missing hostnames and reports via CLI exit path.
  - Serial dev path trims input and rejects empty explicit paths with SERIAL_PORT_PATH_EMPTY_ERROR.
  - MeshInterface _send_packet rejects DB node records lacking numeric num with a descriptive error (MISSING_NODE_NUM_ERROR_TEMPLATE).
  - Tunnel IP/node resolution and payload handling gain length/type checks and safer mapping.
- BLE and reconnection hardening:
  - Guarded retrieval and validation of policy attempt_count values; improved backoff constants and jitter.
  - Discovery/fallback behavior on stale cached addresses and direct-connect failures.
- Resource and shutdown handling:
  - Discovery manager and BLE receive/cleanup paths tightened (earlier cleanup, raise-if-closing checks).
  - Improved connection shutdown validation and explicit transitions during exception paths.
- Logging and diagnostics:
  - Widespread move from f-strings to parameterized logging and additional warnings when secondary cleanup steps fail (e.g., slog/power logger close).

Refactors and thread-safety
- Node and channel safety:
  - Per-node and per-channel locking (RLocks) introduced; channel reads/writes use locked snapshots and helper functions.
  - MAX_CHANNELS and length limits (MAX_SHORT_NAME_LEN, MAX_RINGTONE_LENGTH, MAX_CANNED_MESSAGE_LENGTH) centralized; setOwner enforces truncation.
  - Defensive read-only accessors added (getChannelCopyByChannelIndex, getChannelCopyByName, getDisabledChannelCopy) while live accessors continue to exist.
  - Metadata handling (position, telemetry, admin session passkeys) moved under per-node locks and lastReceived redaction uses new REDACTED_TEXT/REDACTED_BYTES constants.
- BLE internals and coordination:
  - Discovery pending-task set guarded by a global lock; ThreadCoordinator has debug-time ownership checks.
  - BLE state manager uses immutable transition maps and a locked helper _transition_to_unlocked for transitions.
  - BLE runner tracks per-instance deprecation-warning emission deterministically.
- API hygiene and typing:
  - Some public signatures refined for clarity (Node.requestConfig accepts FieldDescriptor|int; serial stream typing now includes BinaryIO; LogDef field types use ArrowDataTypeLike).
  - Many internal names normalized and compatibility shims (COMPAT_STABLE_SHIM) added to retain previous call-sites.

Tests, CI and tooling
- Large expansion and modernization of unit tests across BLE, TCP, SLog, Tunnel, MeshInterface, Node, main/CLI, OTA, powermon, supported_device, and many integration/smoke tests.
- Tests improved for determinism (monkeypatched timing), stronger concurrency coverage, new fixtures and helpers, and more explicit type hints.
- pytest.ini updated (adds smoke1_destructive marker); minor CI tweak to codecov workflow; Makefile/CI targets adjusted for caches and linting.

Breaking changes / migration notes

issues.title: Refactor BLE interface and add node runtime architecture
issues.body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Overview

This PR refactors the BLE/node integration by introducing a new node_runtime subsystem and moving many node-related responsibilities (channel management, settings, transport, admin handling, content, setURL transactions, and response processing) into focused runtime classes. It also applies a series of small BLE-interface cleanups and adds extensive unit tests across the new runtimes and related behaviors. The changes preserve public APIs via compatibility shims where needed and aim to improve thread-safety, typing, and test coverage.

## Key changes

### Features
- New node_runtime subsystem that centralizes node-facing logic into many focused runtimes:
  - Node/channel management: _NodeChannelLookupRuntime, _NodeChannelNormalizationRuntime, _NodeChannelPresentationRuntime, _NodeChannelExportRuntime
  - Channel bootstrap and requests: _NodeChannelRequestRuntime
  - Settings and admin commands: _NodeSettingsMessageBuilder, _NodeSettingsRuntime, _NodeSettingsResponseRuntime, _NodeAdminCommandRuntime
  - Content (ringtones/canned messages): _NodeContentCacheStore, _NodeContentResponseRuntime, _NodeAdminContentRuntime
  - Admin transport and session/ACK handling: _NodeAdminSessionRuntime, _NodeAdminTransportRuntime, _NodeChannelWriteRuntime, _NodeDeleteChannelRuntime, _NodeAckNakRuntime, _NodePositionTimeCommandRuntime
  - Response handling: _NodeMetadataResponseRuntime, _NodeChannelResponseRuntime
  - SetURL pipeline: full parser, planners (add-only/replace-all), execution engine, rollback engine, transaction coordinator, and cache manager
  - Shared utilities/constants: meshtastic.node_runtime.shared (limits, helper functions, constants)
- New convenience/compatibility shims (snake_case ↔ camelCase) for some exposed operations to preserve backwards compatibility.
- Many new unit test modules covering all new runtimes, setURL flows, response handling, transport behavior, and related integrations.

### Fixes / Behavioral adjustments
- SetURL execution/rollback behavior refined (detailed planning, ordered writes, LoRa handling, and more conservative rollback when writes were not started).
- Channel write/primary-channel encryption mutation includes cache consistency checks and explicit invalidation when local cache changes during writes.
- Content (ringtone/canned) runtime: robust fragment accumulation, terminal response detection, cache promotion, and write chunking for large canned messages.
- Channel response runtime: retry logic for routing errors with a single retry limit and terminal failure handling for repeated failures.
- Several lifecycle/ownership hooks resolved more robustly via a centralized _resolve_hook helper, reducing repeated getattr/callable checks and handling unconfigured mock callables.

### Refactors / style
- Major architectural refactor: splitting responsibilities into the node_runtime package and many internal runtime classes to improve modularity, concurrency handling, and testability.
- BLE interface files: minor formatting and lint-comment relocations in compatibility_service.py, errors.py, interface.py, runner.py — no semantic changes.
- Added select pylint suppressions (unnecessary-ellipsis, redefined-outer-name) where appropriate.
- Minor logic simplifications (stale-client closure guard adjusted, small predicate condensing, removal of cast wrappers).

### Tests
- Extensive new test coverage added for setURL pipeline (parser, planners, execution, rollback, cache manager, coordinator).
- New test suites for channel export, lookup, normalization, presentation, request, content runtime, response runtime, settings runtime, transport runtime, and lifecycle controller.
- Existing tests updated (test_mesh_interface.py, test_node.py) to reflect refactored internals and snapshot-based channel-write semantics.

## Breaking changes / migration notes

- No public API signature changes were introduced: exported/public function and class signatures remain unchanged, and compatibility aliases were added where camelCase → snake_case differences exist. The refactor is internal and aims to be backward-compatible.
- Consumers relying on internal, undocumented behaviors could observe differences in timing or caching semantics due to stricter snapshotting and cache-consistency checks; if tests or integrations relied on in-place mutation of cached channel objects, update to use the runtime snapshot-based accessors (the new internals favor defensive copies under locks).
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
issues.display_title: Refactor BLE interface and add node runtime architecture

issues.title: Refactor codebase with constants, sync, and test improvements
issues.body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Overview

This PR is a coordinated refactor that improves thread-safety, validation, and robustness across the codebase and substantially expands unit test coverage. It centralizes many magic numbers into documented constants, tightens locking and input validation (notably for Node/channel state, BLE flows, TCP/stream writes, OTA transfers, serial handling, and tunnel/IP mapping), and preserves backwards compatibility by adding canonical CamelCase names while keeping snake_case shims. Tests and test tooling were modernized for deterministic timing, better fixtures, and broader concurrency/edge-case coverage.

## Key changes

Features
- New module-level constants for timeouts, sizes and limits (examples: OTA_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, OTA_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES, FILE_HASH_READ_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES, SERVICE_CHARACTERISTIC_RETRY_DELAY, EMPTY_READ_MAX_DELAY, TRANSIENT_READ_MAX_DELAY, WRITE_PROGRESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, MAX_CHANNELS, MAX_SHORT_NAME_LEN, METADATA_STDOUT_COMPAT_WAIT_SECONDS, LOG_DIR_COLLISION_MAX_RETRIES, SERIAL_PORT_PATH_EMPTY_ERROR, UNKNOWN_SNR_QUARTER_DB, WRITE_TIMEOUT_ERROR/WRITE_NO_PROGRESS_ERROR).
- OTA: streaming/chunked transfer, file-hashing helpers (hashBytes/hashHex) and compatibility aliases; socket-timeout and chunk-size constants; progress callback support and parameterized logging.
- BLE: retry pacing for characteristic checks, discovery-factory validation with richer errors, discovery fallback when direct connect fails (including device-not-found detection), notification registration retries for BlueZ "notify acquired" conflicts with graceful fallback to polling, and guarded reconnection semantics.
- TCP / stream: partial-send loop using select, write-progress deadlines, unified STREAM_IO_EXCEPTIONS, and explicit write-timeout/error messages to avoid stalled writes.
- Public compatibility surface: many CamelCase canonical names added with snake_case aliases retained (examples: getActiveVersion/get_active_version, detectWindowsPort/detect_windows_port, analysis/powermon/slog aliases).
- Test tooling: new CLI helpers (runCliWithTimeout/runCliArgvWithTimeout/_quote_shell_path), pytest marker smoke1_destructive, and new test fixtures/helpers (ble_client, notification_manager, build_interface, _managed_tunnel, and others).

Fixes and robustness improvements
- Stronger validation and clearer errors:
  - Host/port parsing rejects empty/missing hostnames and reports via CLI exit path.
  - Serial dev path trims input and rejects empty explicit paths with SERIAL_PORT_PATH_EMPTY_ERROR.
  - MeshInterface _send_packet rejects DB node records lacking numeric num with a descriptive error (MISSING_NODE_NUM_ERROR_TEMPLATE).
  - Tunnel IP/node resolution and payload handling gain length/type checks and safer mapping.
- BLE and reconnection hardening:
  - Guarded retrieval and validation of policy attempt_count values; improved backoff constants and jitter.
  - Discovery/fallback behavior on stale cached addresses and direct-connect failures.
- Resource and shutdown handling:
  - Discovery manager and BLE receive/cleanup paths tightened (earlier cleanup, raise-if-closing checks).
  - Improved connection shutdown validation and explicit transitions during exception paths.
- Logging and diagnostics:
  - Widespread move from f-strings to parameterized logging and additional warnings when secondary cleanup steps fail (e.g., slog/power logger close).

Refactors and thread-safety
- Node and channel safety:
  - Per-node and per-channel locking (RLocks) introduced; channel reads/writes use locked snapshots and helper functions.
  - MAX_CHANNELS and length limits (MAX_SHORT_NAME_LEN, MAX_RINGTONE_LENGTH, MAX_CANNED_MESSAGE_LENGTH) centralized; setOwner enforces truncation.
  - Defensive read-only accessors added (getChannelCopyByChannelIndex, getChannelCopyByName, getDisabledChannelCopy) while live accessors continue to exist.
  - Metadata handling (position, telemetry, admin session passkeys) moved under per-node locks and lastReceived redaction uses new REDACTED_TEXT/REDACTED_BYTES constants.
- BLE internals and coordination:
  - Discovery pending-task set guarded by a global lock; ThreadCoordinator has debug-time ownership checks.
  - BLE state manager uses immutable transition maps and a locked helper _transition_to_unlocked for transitions.
  - BLE runner tracks per-instance deprecation-warning emission deterministically.
- API hygiene and typing:
  - Some public signatures refined for clarity (Node.requestConfig accepts FieldDescriptor|int; serial stream typing now includes BinaryIO; LogDef field types use ArrowDataTypeLike).
  - Many internal names normalized and compatibility shims (COMPAT_STABLE_SHIM) added to retain previous call-sites.

Tests, CI and tooling
- Large expansion and modernization of unit tests across BLE, TCP, SLog, Tunnel, MeshInterface, Node, main/CLI, OTA, powermon, supported_device, and many integration/smoke tests.
- Tests improved for determinism (monkeypatched timing), stronger concurrency coverage, new fixtures and helpers, and more explicit type hints.
- pytest.ini updated (adds smoke1_destructive marker); minor CI tweak to codecov workflow; Makefile/CI targets adjusted for caches and linting.

Breaking changes / migration notes
- No deliberate breaking API removals detected; the PR favors adding canonical CamelCase names while keeping snake_case compatibility aliases.
- Migration considerations:
  - External code that relied on internal implementation details, private flags, or specific timing/side-effect ordering may need adjustment because many internals were renamed, encapsulated, or had behavior tightened.
  - Tests and tooling that depended on previous timing behavior should adopt the new deterministic fixtures/helpers (e.g., runCliWithTimeout/run_cli_with_timeout and the new BLE/test fixtures).
  - Consumers that tuned behavior via hard-coded values should switch to the newly exposed constants for configuration where appropriate.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
issues.display_title: Refactor codebase with constants, sync, and test improvements

Co-authored-by: Armand <armand@synaptent.com>

Merge pull request #8 from ctmaomao/codex/t4-freshness-status-system

[codex] add T4 freshness / stale / degraded status system

Merge pull request #5171 from njfio/codex/issue-3956-rotation-freshness-enforcement

feat: enforce rotation freshness stale-key fail-closed outcomes (#3956)

feat(F-015): Learning decay and freshness

Staleness detection, freshness scoring, review prompts,
and reconfirmation for learned patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(02-01): add CSS classes for freshness signals and run nav

- .client-last-tested: 11px gray metadata below client name
- .freshness-badge / .freshness-stale: amber pill for stale data
- .run-nav / .run-nav-label / .run-select: run selector styling
commits.display_title: feat(02-01): add CSS classes for freshness signals and run nav

Initial flywheel CLI v0.1.0

- sync: extract [LEARNING]/[UPDATE]/[STALE] tags from agent memory logs
- freshness: staleness report for vault notes
- verify: mark notes as current
- Zero runtime deps beyond obsidian-cli
- Go binary, ~15KB stripped

{"issues": [{"url": "https://github.com/EvanderLatine/home_server/pull/2", "title": "feat(maintenance): raise claude-home wait timeout to 10min + status probes", "issue_number": 2, "body": "## Summary
- `WAIT_FOR_START_SEC` defaults lifted to **600s** (was 180 in `run.sh`, 300 in `postboot.sh`).
- New `WAIT_STATUS_INTERVAL_SEC=30` → periodic `Waiting for claude-home.service: elapsed=Ns active=… tmux=…` log line during the wait loop.
- On timeout, `start_assistant_service` now dumps `systemctl status` + last 3 min of `journalctl -u claude-home.service` + tmux pane snapshot to `${RUN_DIR}/start-timeout-status.log`.
- Adds `patches/maintenance-wait-timeout-10min.sh` (idempotent installer with `--dry-run`/`--no-backup` + post-install validation) and `patches/MAINTENANCE_WAIT_TIMEOUT_10MIN_GUIDE.md` (apply / validate / rollback).

## Motivation
Maintenance run `20260416T020000Z-dff1eece` (2026-04-16 05:00 MSK) succeeded through APT + toolchain reconcile (17 pkgs, Codex 0.120.0 → 0.121.0, Gemini 0.38.0 → 0.38.1). `claude-home.service` then took ~271s after `systemctl start` to come back, exceeding the old 180s `WAIT_FOR_START_SEC` default. The trap finalized the run with `exit_code=1` and sent two `[CRITICAL]/[ERROR]` Telegram alerts even though the service was about to come up. This PR widens the headroom and adds self-diagnostics so the next borderline case is debuggable from a single file.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash -n scripts/assistant-maintenance-run.sh` + `scripts/assistant-maintenance-postboot.sh`
- [x] `bash patches/maintenance-wait-timeout-10min.sh --dry-run` prints the expected diff against `/usr/local/sbin/*`
- [x] Installer verifies patched source (`grep` + `bash -n`), backs up target, re-validates installed file, `systemctl daemon-reload`, and confirms `WAIT_FOR_START_SEC:-600` ended up on both targets
- [ ] End-to-end: `sudo systemctl start assistant-maintenance-run.service` → confirm `Starting claude-home.service (wait timeout: 600s, status probe every 30s)` + periodic `Waiting for…` lines in `/var/log/assistant-maintenance/<run-id>.log` (to be run on the host)
- [ ] Confirm no regression in next scheduled 05:00 MSK cycle

## Notes
- No `assistant-maintenance-run.service` / `.timer` unit changes — `TimeoutStartSec=12h` already has plenty of headroom for the new 600s wait.
- Env overrides remain available via `/etc/default/assistant-maintenance` or `systemctl edit`.
- If 10 min ever proves insufficient, don't reflexively bump — read `start-timeout-status.log` first.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)", "state": "open", "user": {"login": "EvanderLatine", "name": "EvanderLatine", "email": null, "avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/43015355?v=4", "id": 43015355}, "assignees": null, "labels": null, "milestone": null, "state_reason": null, "comments": 0, "created_at": "2026-04-16T08:20:31Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-16T08:20:31Z", "closed_at": null, "display_url": "https://github.com/EvanderLatine/home_server/pull/2", "display_title": "feat(maintenance): raise claude-home wait timeout to 10min + status probes"}, {"url": "https://github.com/EvanderLatine/home_server/pull/1", "title": "Maintenance robustness P0/P1: OOB alerting, atomic state, deferred wipe, watchdog (3-agent consensus)", "issue_number": 1, "body": "## Summary

Implements P0/P1 maintenance robustness fixes from a 3-agent consensus audit (Claude + Codex + Gemini, 3 rounds, 11 findings + 3 new). Score before: **5.67/10** (strong happy-path, fragile degradation paths). Full audit lives at `/home/aiagent/assistant/git/consensus-maintenance-audit/round3-revalidation.md`.

**Three architectural shifts:**
1. **Out-of-band Telegram alerting** via direct `curl` (`scripts/maintenance-alert-direct.sh`) — critical alerts no longer depend on Claude/tmux being healthy. Closes the Ouroboros (F1).
2. **Atomic state writes** — `write_run_state` now goes through `run_jq_to_file` (mktemp + rename + size check), no more zero-byte landmines if jq crashes mid-write. Closes F4/N3.
3. **Deferred digest archive** — `archive_and_reset_digest` moved from before `apt` to after a successful run. No more lost ledger on apt failure. Closes F3.

**Plus:** apt `--simulate` preflight blocks critical-package removals (F7) • `CODEX_VERSION`/`GEMINI_VERSION` env-pin (F8) • hourly cadence watchdog with reboot-safe dedup state (F9) • postboot tmux pane guard (F5) • pre-notify failure now demands OOB alert success or aborts (F6) • `MAINTENANCE_TRIGGER` env var for honest manual-vs-timer provenance (F10) • OOB alerts on `start_assistant_service` failures (F11) • install-time drift check (N1) • symmetric defensive coding on degraded paths (N2).

## Files

| Type | Path |
|------|------|
| ADD | `docs/MAINTENANCE_ROBUSTNESS_FIXES.md` |
| ADD | `scripts/maintenance-alert-direct.sh` |
| ADD | `scripts/assistant-maintenance-watchdog.sh` |
| ADD | `systemd/assistant-maintenance-watchdog.{service,timer}` |
| MOD | `scripts/assistant-maintenance-run.sh` (+233/-23) |
| MOD | `scripts/assistant-maintenance-postboot.sh` (+72) |
| MOD | `scripts/install-assistant-maintenance.sh` (+108/-1) |
| MOD | `scripts/maintenance-smoke-test.sh` (+184) |
| MOD | `templates/assistant-maintenance.env` |
| MOD | `systemd/assistant-maintenance-run.service` |

12 files, **+1507/-35**, 11 commits.

## How it was built

- **Claude** wrote the spec (round 3 revalidation against the canonical `assistant/git/home_server/`, after the obsolete `/home/aiagent/home_server/` bundle was deleted).
- **Codex** (GPT-5.x) implemented all 11 commits via `codex exec --sandbox workspace-write` from a single delegation prompt.
- **Gemini 3.1 Pro** reviewed the diff via `gemini-agent.sh review` and verdicted **APPROVE WITH NITS** — the one nit (watchdog dedup state on tmpfs) was fixed in commit `4b584b5` before push.
- **Claude** ran the final smoke test (9/9 PASS) and opened this PR.

## Test plan

- [x] `bash -n` on every modified script
- [x] `systemd-analyze verify` on modified units
- [x] `bash scripts/maintenance-smoke-test.sh` — 9/9 sections green, including atomic-write fault injection, OOB alert without BOT_TOKEN, apt preflight blocking critical removal, postboot tmux guard with `vim` pane, watchdog stale-run alert
- [ ] Yaroslav reviews diff
- [ ] Yaroslav merges
- [ ] Yaroslav reruns `sudo ./scripts/install-assistant-maintenance.sh` to deploy + populate `BOT_TOKEN`/`ADMIN_CHAT_ID` in `/etc/default/assistant-maintenance`
- [ ] Yaroslav runs one manual maintenance to validate OOB alert path with token set, then with token unset (graceful fallback to log)
- [ ] Yaroslav verifies `cmp` between canonical scripts and `/usr/local/sbin/` shows zero drift after install rerun

## Rollback

`git revert` the PR + rerun installer. Watchdog timer can be disabled via `sudo systemctl disable --now assistant-maintenance-watchdog.timer`. State files in `/home/aiagent/assistant/state/maintenance/` are backwards-compatible (no schema changes to `run.tson`).

- Renamed all column display names from `snake_case` to `Business Friendly Title Case` across all 7 table TMDL files
- `sourceColumn` values are **unchanged** (preserves Gold Layer OneLake shortcut compatibility)
- Updated all cascading references: DAX measures, calculated columns, relationships, and RLS expressions

## Files changed (8)
| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `Orders.tmdl` | 40 columns + 4 calc columns + 16 measures (DAX refs) |
| `Notifications.tmdl` | 30 columns + 3 measures (DAX refs) |
| `CriticalAssets.tmdl` | 18 columns |

| `roles.tmdl` | RLS filter expressions (SiteManager role) |

## Naming conventions
- Prepositions lowercase: `for`, `of`, `the`, `in`, `on`, `at`, `by`
- Abbreviations uppercase: `PM`, `MRP`
- Expanded abbreviations: `prodn_superv` -> `Production Supervisor`, `order_type_desc` -> `Order Type Description`
- DateDim columns unchanged (already PascalCase)
- Calculated columns unchanged (already PascalCase: `IsOnTime`, `AssetClass`, etc.)

## Example
```tmdl
// BEFORE:
column maintenance_order_number
    sourceColumn: maintenance_order_number

// AFTER:
column 'Maintenance Order Number'
    sourceColumn: maintenance_order_number
```

- Removed two top-level `//` comment lines from `relationships.tmdl` that caused TMDL parsing failure

- Removed `// Active relationships (7)` (line 1) and `// Inactive relationships (4)` (line 31)
- Verified no other TMDL files have top-level `//` comments (`grep ^// *.tmdl` returns zero matches)

- **Previous fix (PR #5)** changed `///` to `//` in `expressions.tmdl`, but Power BI Desktop still fails because TMDL files cannot contain only comments — the parser requires a valid object declaration on the first non-blank line
- **This fix** deletes `expressions.tmdl` entirely since there are no shared expressions in the stub data model
- TMDL only requires files for objects that are actually defined; an empty/comment-only file is invalid per the TMDL spec

## Test plan
- [ ] Open `TevaPlantMaintenance.pbip` in Power BI Desktop and verify it loads without TMDL parsing errors
- [ ] Verify all tables, relationships, and measures are present in the model

issues.title: feat(maintenance): raise claude-home wait timeout to 10min + status probes
issues.body:
## Summary
- `WAIT_FOR_START_SEC` defaults lifted to **600s** (was 180 in `run.sh`, 300 in `postboot.sh`).
- New `WAIT_STATUS_INTERVAL_SEC=30` → periodic `Waiting for claude-home.service: elapsed=Ns active=… tmux=…` log line during the wait loop.
- On timeout, `start_assistant_service` now dumps `systemctl status` + last 3 min of `journalctl -u claude-home.service` + tmux pane snapshot to `${RUN_DIR}/start-timeout-status.log`.
- Adds `patches/maintenance-wait-timeout-10min.sh` (idempotent installer with `--dry-run`/`--no-backup` + post-install validation) and `patches/MAINTENANCE_WAIT_TIMEOUT_10MIN_GUIDE.md` (apply / validate / rollback).

## Motivation
Maintenance run `20260416T020000Z-dff1eece` (2026-04-16 05:00 MSK) succeeded through APT + toolchain reconcile (17 pkgs, Codex 0.120.0 → 0.121.0, Gemini 0.38.0 → 0.38.1). `claude-home.service` then took ~271s after `systemctl start` to come back, exceeding the old 180s `WAIT_FOR_START_SEC` default. The trap finalized the run with `exit_code=1` and sent two `[CRITICAL]/[ERROR]` Telegram alerts even though the service was about to come up. This PR widens the headroom and adds self-diagnostics so the next borderline case is debuggable from a single file.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash -n scripts/assistant-maintenance-run.sh` + `scripts/assistant-maintenance-postboot.sh`
- [x] `bash patches/maintenance-wait-timeout-10min.sh --dry-run` prints the expected diff against `/usr/local/sbin/*`
- [x] Installer verifies patched source (`grep` + `bash -n`), backs up target, re-validates installed file, `systemctl daemon-reload`, and confirms `WAIT_FOR_START_SEC:-600` ended up on both targets
- [ ] End-to-end: `sudo systemctl start assistant-maintenance-run.service` → confirm `Starting claude-home.service (wait timeout: 600s, status probe every 30s)` + periodic `Waiting for…` lines in `/var/log/assistant-maintenance/<run-id>.log` (to be run on the host)
- [ ] Confirm no regression in next scheduled 05:00 MSK cycle

## Notes
- No `assistant-maintenance-run.service` / `.timer` unit changes — `TimeoutStartSec=12h` already has plenty of headroom for the new 600s wait.
- Env overrides remain available via `/etc/default/assistant-maintenance` or `systemctl edit`.
- If 10 min ever proves insufficient, don't reflexively bump — read `start-timeout-status.log` first.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Maintenance robustness P0/P1: OOB alerting, atomic state, deferred wipe, watchdog (3-agent consensus)
issues.body:
## Summary

Implements P0/P1 maintenance robustness fixes from a 3-agent consensus audit (Claude + Codex + Gemini, 3 rounds, 11 findings + 3 new). Score before: **5.67/10** (strong happy-path, fragile degradation paths). Full audit lives at `/home/aiagent/assistant/git/consensus-maintenance-audit/round3-revalidation.md`.

**Three architectural shifts:**
1. **Out-of-band Telegram alerting** via direct `curl` (`scripts/maintenance-alert-direct.sh`) — critical alerts no longer depend on Claude/tmux being healthy. Closes the Ouroboros (F1).
2. **Atomic state writes** — `write_run_state` now goes through `run_jq_to_file` (mktemp + rename + size check), no more zero-byte landmines if jq crashes mid-write. Closes F4/N3.
3. **Deferred digest archive** — `archive_and_reset_digest` moved from before `apt` to after a successful run. No more lost ledger on apt failure. Closes F3.

**Plus:** apt `--simulate` preflight blocks critical-package removals (F7) • `CODEX_VERSION`/`GEMINI_VERSION` env-pin (F8) • hourly cadence watchdog with reboot-safe dedup state (F9) • postboot tmux pane guard (F5) • pre-notify failure now demands OOB alert success or aborts (F6) • `MAINTENANCE_TRIGGER` env var for honest manual-vs-timer provenance (F10) • OOB alerts on `start_assistant_service` failures (F11) • install-time drift check (N1) • symmetric defensive coding on degraded paths (N2).

## Files

| Type | Path |
|------|------|
| ADD | `docs/MAINTENANCE_ROBUSTNESS_FIXES.md` |
| ADD | `scripts/maintenance-alert-direct.sh` |
| ADD | `scripts/assistant-maintenance-watchdog.sh` |
| ADD | `systemd/assistant-maintenance-watchdog.{service,timer}` |
| MOD | `scripts/assistant-maintenance-run.sh` (+233/-23) |
| MOD | `scripts/assistant-maintenance-postboot.sh` (+72) |
| MOD | `scripts/install-assistant-maintenance.sh` (+108/-1) |
| MOD | `scripts/maintenance-smoke-test.sh` (+184) |
| MOD | `templates/assistant-maintenance.env` |
| MOD | `systemd/assistant-maintenance-run.service` |

12 files, **+1507/-35**, 11 commits.

## How it was built

- **Claude** wrote the spec (round 3 revalidation against the canonical `assistant/git/home_server/`, after the obsolete `/home/aiagent/home_server/` bundle was deleted).
- **Codex** (GPT-5.x) implemented all 11 commits via `codex exec --sandbox workspace-write` from a single delegation prompt.
- **Gemini 3.1 Pro** reviewed the diff via `gemini-agent.sh review` and verdicted **APPROVE WITH NITS** — the one nit (watchdog dedup state on tmpfs) was fixed in commit `4b584b5` before push.
- **Claude** ran the final smoke test (9/9 PASS) and opened this PR.

## Test plan

- [x] `bash -n` on every modified script
- [x] `systemd-analyze verify` on modified units
- [x] `bash scripts/maintenance-smoke-test.sh` — 9/9 sections green, including atomic-write fault injection, OOB alert without BOT_TOKEN, apt preflight blocking critical removal, postboot tmux guard with `vim` pane, watchdog stale-run alert
- [ ] Yaroslav reviews diff
- [ ] Yaroslav merges
- [ ] Yaroslav reruns `sudo ./scripts/install-assistant-maintenance.sh` to deploy + populate `BOT_TOKEN`/`ADMIN_CHAT_ID` in `/etc/default/assistant-maintenance`
- [ ] Yaroslav runs one manual maintenance to validate OOB alert path with token set, then with token unset (graceful fallback to log)
- [ ] Yaroslav verifies `cmp` between canonical scripts and `/usr/local/sbin/` shows zero drift after install rerun

## Rollback

`git revert` the PR + rerun installer. Watchdog timer can be disabled via `sudo systemctl disable --now assistant-maintenance-watchdog.timer`. State files in `/home/aiagent/assistant/state/maintenance/` are backwards-compatible (no schema changes to `run.tson`).

🤖 Generated by Claude + Codex + Gemini consensus pipeline

issues.title: [Phase 3] Rename all column display names to Business Friendly Title Case
issues.body:
## Summary
- Renamed all column display names from `snake_case` to `Business Friendly Title Case` across all 7 table TMDL files
- `sourceColumn` values are **unchanged** (preserves Gold Layer OneLake shortcut compatibility)
- Updated all cascading references: DAX measures, calculated columns, relationships, and RLS expressions

## Files changed (8)
| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `Orders.tmdl` | 40 columns + 4 calc columns + 16 measures (DAX refs) |
| `Notifications.tmdl` | 30 columns + 3 measures (DAX refs) |
| `CriticalAssets.tmdl` | 18 columns |
| `OperatingHours.tmdl` | 6 columns |
| `OperatingWeeks.tmdl` | 7 columns |
| `RLS_Config.tmdl` | 3 columns |
| `relationships.tmdl` | All 11 fromColumn/toColumn refs |
| `roles.tmdl` | RLS filter expressions (SiteManager role) |

## Naming conventions
- Prepositions lowercase: `for`, `of`, `the`, `in`, `on`, `at`, `by`
- Abbreviations uppercase: `PM`, `MRP`
- Expanded abbreviations: `prodn_superv` -> `Production Supervisor`, `order_type_desc` -> `Order Type Description`
- DateDim columns unchanged (already PascalCase)
- Calculated columns unchanged (already PascalCase: `IsOnTime`, `AssetClass`, etc.)

## Example
```tmdl
// BEFORE:
column maintenance_order_number
    sourceColumn: maintenance_order_number

// AFTER:
column 'Maintenance Order Number'
    sourceColumn: maintenance_order_number
```

## Test plan
- [ ] Open `TevaPlantMaintenance.pbip` in Power BI Desktop — verify no errors
- [ ] Verify column names display as Business Friendly Title Case in field list
- [ ] Verify all measures calculate correctly with new column references
- [ ] Verify RLS roles filter correctly with new column names

issues.title: [HC3] Remove top-level comments from relationships.tmdl
issues.body:
## Summary
- Removed two top-level `//` comment lines from `relationships.tmdl` that caused TMDL parsing failure
- **Root cause:** TMDL does not allow `//` comments at root indentation level (indent 0) — the parser expects every non-blank root line to be a valid object declaration
- Removed `// Active relationships (7)` (line 1) and `// Inactive relationships (4)` (line 31)
- Verified no other TMDL files have top-level `//` comments (`grep ^// *.tmdl` returns zero matches)

## Test plan
- [ ] Open `TevaPlantMaintenance.pbip` in Power BI Desktop and verify it loads without TMDL parsing errors
- [ ] Verify all 11 relationships (7 active + 4 inactive) are present in the model

issues.title: [HC3] Delete expressions.tmdl — empty TMDL files are invalid
issues.body:
## Summary
- **Previous fix (PR #5)** changed `///` to `//` in `expressions.tmdl`, but Power BI Desktop still fails because TMDL files cannot contain only comments — the parser requires a valid object declaration on the first non-blank line
- **This fix** deletes `expressions.tmdl` entirely since there are no shared expressions in the stub data model
- TMDL only requires files for objects that are actually defined; an empty/comment-only file is invalid per the TMDL spec

## Test plan

issues.title: [T1.1-T1.7] Phase 1: Generate stub data M code for all 7 tables
issues.body:
## Summary

- Generates complete Power Query M code (`let...in` blocks) for all 7 tables as standalone `.m` files in `mcode-staging/`
- Follows DATA_GENERATION_PLAN v2.0 exactly: column names, types, value domains, generation strategies, row counts
- All FK references are valid: equipment numbers EQ-00001..EQ-00020, plant codes IL01/CA01/AU01
- Nullable columns use `type nullable date` / `type nullable text` as required

## Files created

| File | Rows | Columns |
|---|---|---|
| `mcode-staging/DateDim.m` | 2557 | 14 |
| `mcode-staging/CriticalAssets.m` | 20 | 18 |
| `mcode-staging/RLS_Config.m` | 6 | 3 |
| `mcode-staging/Orders.m` | 50 | 79 |
| `mcode-staging/Notifications.m` | 60 | 41 |
| `mcode-staging/OperatingHours.m` | 240 | 6 |
| `mcode-staging/OperatingWeeks.m` | 1040 | 7 |

## Key implementation notes

- **Orders**: 6 nullable columns (`actual_finish_date`, `technical_completion_date`, `close_date`, `released`, `confirmed_order_finish_date`, `date_of_technical_inspection`) — all use `type nullable date`
- **Notifications**: 3 nullable columns (`notification_malfunction_start_date`, `notification_malfunction_end_date` as `type nullable date`; `notification_breakdown_duration` as `type nullable text`); `maintenance_order_number` is `type nullable text` with 10% nulls (`Number.Mod(i, 10) = 0`)
- **OperatingWeeks**: week anchor is `Date.AddDays(Date.StartOfWeek(#date(2025,1,1), Day.Monday), 7)` = Jan 6 2025, ensuring all 52 weeks stay within DateDim range
- All deterministic generation — modular arithmetic on index, no randomness

## Test plan

- [ ] Paste each `.m` file into a Power BI Desktop blank query and verify it refreshes without errors
- [ ] Confirm row counts: DateDim=2557, CriticalAssets=20, RLS_Config=6, Orders=50, Notifications=60, OperatingHours=240, OperatingWeeks=1040
- [ ] Verify FK integrity: Orders/Notifications equipment numbers all resolve to CriticalAssets
- [ ] Verify nullable columns accept null values without type errors
- [ ] Confirm all date values fall within DateDim range (2020-01-01..2026-12-31)

issues.title: Возможные улучшения
issues.body:
- Воспроизводимость и окружение: убрать ! pip install ... из ноутбука и закрепить зависимости в da_practicum_env.yml/requirements.txt. Иначе результаты нестабильны и зависят от окружения.
- Контроль случайности: у вас задан RANDOM_STATE, но стоит явно зафиксировать numpy и random (а также передать random_state во все модели/поисковые процедуры, где это возможно), чтобы воспроизведение было 1:1.
- Качество данных: в выводах есть допущение о «неверной» шкале прибыли и ручное масштабирование. Это место лучше обосновать: либо проверка источника, либо корректировка через правила валидации (иначе риск неверных бизнес-выводов).
- Метрики и пороги: ROC-AUC хорош для сравнения, но для бизнеса обычно нужен баланс precision/recall и выбор рабочего порога (например, maximize F1 или ожидаемую выгоду). Добавьте матрицу ошибок и анализ порога.
- Дисбаланс классов: если есть дисбаланс целевого признака, полезно показать доли классов и добавить PR-AUC или balanced accuracy.
- Базовая линия: включить DummyClassifier как baseline — это быстро показывает ценность модели.
- Проверка утечек: убедиться, что признаки, агрегированные «по последнему месяцу», не используют данные после целевого события.
- Интерпретация признаков: для KNN SHAP через KernelExplainer дорог и может быть нестабилен. Можно добавить permutation importance или SHAP на другом модельном классе (например, деревья) для сравнения.
- Сегментация: если использовали KMeans, стоит явно обосновать k (elbow/silhouette/Calinski-Harabasz), а также показать устойчивость кластеров и профили сегментов в метриках.
- Фичи “динамики”: добавить темпы изменений (например, текущий/предыдущий месяц выручки, изменение времени на сайте) — часто дают дополнительную предсказательную силу.
- Качество текста/формулировок: в Markdown много опечаток и нечетких формулировок. Исправления помогут читабельности и восприятию результатов.

- seed realtime thread history through a completed local state DB home
- assert seeded history is visible through the production thread store path
- improve startup-context assertion diagnostics without weakening coverage

## What was wrong
The affected test verifies that realtime conversation startup injects prior thread history into startup context.

The old setup created thread history, but it did not fully mirror the production persistence state expected by the realtime startup reader. In particular, the synthetic state could exist without the local SQLite/backfill readiness conditions that the production read path depends on.

That meant the test was not only checking whether realtime startup includes history. It was also accidentally checking whether the test's partially initialized thread-store state happened to look ready enough to the production reader.

## Why it was flaky
The failure mode was order/readiness-sensitive.

Sometimes the seeded history was visible through the production read path by the time realtime startup looked for it. Other times the reader behaved as if the relevant persisted history was unavailable, because the test had not completed the same local state setup that production expects.

That made the assertion depend on incidental persistence readiness instead of the semantic contract: if prior thread history exists in the production store path, realtime startup should include it.

## How this fixes it
The change makes the test setup match the read path being tested.

- It marks SQLite backfill complete before constructing/running the scenario.
- It verifies seeded history through `LocalThreadStore`, which is the production-style path realtime startup uses.
- It adds focused startup-context assertion helpers so the test checks the semantic output directly and produces better diagnostics when it fails.

With the persistence state initialized the way the reader expects, the test deterministically validates realtime startup history injection instead of depending on partial store readiness.

## Validation
- Remote Bazel before final review loop: `//codex-rs/core:core-all-test --test_arg=suite::realtime_conversation::conversation_start_injects_startup_context_from_thread_history --test_arg=--exact` passed on `codex-flaky-rt-0415`
- Read-only subagent review loop converged with no remaining findings

Co-authored-by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

This PR prepares Scholr for the cheapest practical deployment path while preserving the current MVP flow.

It keeps the existing product behavior intact:
- Research
- Notes
- Doubt
- SSE streaming
- Dashboard history
- local vs production env behavior

## What changed

### Deployment readiness
- switched deployment docs from Railway to Render for the backend
- kept Vercel as the frontend target
- updated backend Procfile for Render start command
- added `backend/runtime.txt`
- added `frontend/.env.example`
- updated env examples for Render + Vercel

### Repo safety and hygiene
- removed tracked `backend/.env`
- confirmed env files, DB files, caches, venv, `.next`, and `node_modules` are ignored
- confirmed frontend is not a nested git repo or submodule
- removed stray frontend repo boilerplate files that did not belong in the public project view

### GitHub presentation
- upgraded `README.md`
- updated `PROJECT_PROGRESS.md`
- updated `DEPLOY_CHECKLIST.md` into a Render-first, Vercel-second runbook

### Stability validation
- frontend lint passed
- frontend typecheck passed
- backend compile/import checks passed
- tracked-file secret scan passed

## Build note

`npm run build` compiles successfully, then ends with Windows `spawn EPERM` locally after the compile/typecheck phase.

This appears to be a local Windows process issue, not an app-code build failure.

issues.title: research: Replayable Agent Runtimes
issues.body:
## Summary

Adds a research article on replayable agent runtimes and event-sourced execution histories for production AI agents.

Covers:
- why LLM tracing alone is not enough for long-running agents
- how durable execution, checkpoints, event histories, and deterministic replay apply to agent runtimes
- the relationship between OpenTelemetry/OpenInference traces and authoritative execution logs
- how replayable traces become recovery, audit, and regression-testing infrastructure

## Verification

- npm run build
issues.display_title: research: Replayable Agent Runtimes

issues.title: fix: prevent crash when printing with right sidebar open
issues.body:
## Summary
- Added `isPrinting` check using `useMediaQuery("print")` to conditionally unmount the aside sidebar's children during print, preventing a crash when the comments or history panel is open while printing
- When `window.print()` is triggered with the aside sidebar open, the browser's `matchMedia("print")` change event fires and React's microtask scheduler invalidates pending callbacks in child components (`ResizeObserver` in `Scrollable`, `useMeasure` in `ResizingHeightContainer`), resulting in an `ErrorBoundary` error: "The provided callback is no longer runnable"
- Also refactored `useMediaQuery` to defer state updates via `setTimeout(0)` to avoid microtask conflicts during print transitions — this fix is consistent with the existing `useMediaQuery("print")` pattern used in `PageScroll.tsx` and `useBuildTheme.ts`, and the `@media print { display: none }` CSS rule matches the left sidebar's print-hiding approach in `Sidebar.tsx`

## Test plan
- [x] Verified printing with comments sidebar open no longer crashes
- [x] Verified printing with history sidebar open no longer crashes
- [x] Verified normal printing (no sidebar open) still works
- [x] Confirmed responsive behavior unchanged (`useMobile` hook still works)
- [x] Confirmed no regressions in `useBuildTheme` print detection
issues.display_title: fix: prevent crash when printing with right sidebar open

issues.title: v0.6.0: Batch API + Phase 2 Knowledge Graphs + Metrics Redesign (complete)
issues.body:
Complete v0.6.0 implementation combining all three major features:

## Batch API (50% cost savings for async)
- Non-interactive workload auto-detection
- Queue management with batch processing
- Result polling with proper job tracking
- Stacks with cache for ~55% combined savings

## Phase 2: Knowledge Graphs
- SQLite-based graph database with AST indexing  
- Web + local file indexing
- Zero-token graph queries (99% savings on relationships)
- Fast traversals (<10ms typical)

## Complete Metrics Redesign
- Label-based cardinality (9 metrics, ~127 combinations vs 1000+)
- Web dashboard API endpoints + CLI viewer
- Prometheus + OpenTelemetry support
- Production-ready alerting and dashboards

✅ All 639+ tests passing
✅ Based on main with full git history
✅ Production-ready for v0.6.0 release

issues.title: test: make realtime startup history deterministic
issues.body:
## Summary
- seed realtime thread history through a completed local state DB home
- assert seeded history is visible through the production thread store path
- improve startup-context assertion diagnostics without weakening coverage

## What was wrong
The affected test verifies that realtime conversation startup injects prior thread history into startup context.

The old setup created thread history, but it did not fully mirror the production persistence state expected by the realtime startup reader. In particular, the synthetic state could exist without the local SQLite/backfill readiness conditions that the production read path depends on.

That meant the test was not only checking whether realtime startup includes history. It was also accidentally checking whether the test's partially initialized thread-store state happened to look ready enough to the production reader.

## Why it was flaky
The failure mode was order/readiness-sensitive.

Sometimes the seeded history was visible through the production read path by the time realtime startup looked for it. Other times the reader behaved as if the relevant persisted history was unavailable, because the test had not completed the same local state setup that production expects.

That made the assertion depend on incidental persistence readiness instead of the semantic contract: if prior thread history exists in the production store path, realtime startup should include it.

## How this fixes it
The change makes the test setup match the read path being tested.

- It marks SQLite backfill complete before constructing/running the scenario.
- It verifies seeded history through `LocalThreadStore`, which is the production-style path realtime startup uses.
- It adds focused startup-context assertion helpers so the test checks the semantic output directly and produces better diagnostics when it fails.

With the persistence state initialized the way the reader expects, the test deterministically validates realtime startup history injection instead of depending on partial store readiness.

## Validation
- Remote Bazel before final review loop: `//codex-rs/core:core-all-test --test_arg=suite::realtime_conversation::conversation_start_injects_startup_context_from_thread_history --test_arg=--exact` passed on `codex-flaky-rt-0415`
- Read-only subagent review loop converged with no remaining findings

Co-authored-by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>
issues.display_title: test: make realtime startup history deterministic

issues.title: Prepare Scholr for Vercel and Render deployment
issues.body:
## Summary

This PR prepares Scholr for the cheapest practical deployment path while preserving the current MVP flow.

It keeps the existing product behavior intact:
- Research
- Notes
- Doubt
- SSE streaming
- Dashboard history
- local vs production env behavior

## What changed

### Deployment readiness
- switched deployment docs from Railway to Render for the backend
- kept Vercel as the frontend target
- updated backend Procfile for Render start command
- added `backend/runtime.txt`
- added `frontend/.env.example`
- updated env examples for Render + Vercel

### Repo safety and hygiene
- removed tracked `backend/.env`
- confirmed env files, DB files, caches, venv, `.next`, and `node_modules` are ignored
- confirmed frontend is not a nested git repo or submodule
- removed stray frontend repo boilerplate files that did not belong in the public project view

### GitHub presentation
- upgraded `README.md`
- updated `PROJECT_PROGRESS.md`
- updated `DEPLOY_CHECKLIST.md` into a Render-first, Vercel-second runbook

### Stability validation
- frontend lint passed
- frontend typecheck passed
- backend compile/import checks passed
- tracked-file secret scan passed

## Build note

`npm run build` compiles successfully, then ends with Windows `spawn EPERM` locally after the compile/typecheck phase.

This appears to be a local Windows process issue, not an app-code build failure.

## Render deployment target

Backend:
- Root Directory: `backend`
- Build Command: `pip install -r requirements.txt`
- Start Command: `uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT`

Required env vars:
- `GEMINI_API_KEY`
- `DATABASE_URL`
- `FRONTEND_URL`
- `ALLOWED_ORIGINS`
- `ALLOWED_ORIGIN_REGEX`

## Vercel deployment target

Frontend:
- Root Directory: `frontend`

Required env var:
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL`

## Notes

- Render free web services may sleep and cold start after inactivity
- Render free filesystem is ephemeral, so SQLite should be treated as local-only
- Prefer Render PostgreSQL through `DATABASE_URL` for production history persistence
issues.display_title: Prepare Scholr for Vercel and Render deployment

issues.title: test(PN-19561): add test events mixpanel for Onboarding flow
issues.body:
Adds Mixpanel triggerEvent assertions to the onboarding test suite in pn-personafisica-webapp. Previously, component tests verified UI behaviour and API calls but never asserted that analytics events were actually fired.

### Changes
- OnboardingHome.test.tsx (new file) — covers the home screen view event on mount, the flow-selection event for each card (SEND, Courtesy, IO), and the exit-flow event.
- EmailStep.test.tsx — asserts EMAIL_VERIFICATION, EMAIL_ACTIVATED, EMAIL_EDITING, EMAIL_CONFIRMED, and EMAIL_OTP (OTP dialog opened) across the relevant existing tests. Also adds a new test for the pending-verification scenario.
- IoStep.test.tsx — asserts the screen-view event on each IO status (unavailable / disabled / enabled) and the action events: IO_DOWNLOAD_SELECTED, IO_DOWNLOAD_VERIFICATION, IO_ACTIVATION_SELECTED, IO_ACTIVATED, IO_CONFIRMED.
- DigitalDomicileWizard.test.tsx — asserts SERCQ_ACTIVATION on mount, SERCQ_SEND_SELECTED and EMAIL_ACTIVATION when the SEND path is chosen, BACK_SELECTED with the correct screen, and the end-of-flow events (IO_DOWNLOAD_DECLINED, UX_CONVERSION, UX_SUCCESS) in both the SEND and PEC full-flow tests.

### Approach
PFEventStrategyFactory is mocked at module level in each test file via vi.mock. The mock is reset with vi.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach so each test gets a clean call history. No production code was changed.

-"""YouTube Reporting API client for the KPI dashboard.
-
-Why a separate path from `ingest/youtube_client.py`:
-The v2 Analytics `reports.query()` endpoint does not expose channel-level
-`impressions` / `impressionsClickThroughRate` for non-content-owner channels
-(Google issuetracker #254665034). The Reporting API (`youtubereporting v1`)
-delivers daily CSV reports that DO include card-level impression data via
-the `channel_cards_a1` system-managed report.
-
-How it works:
-1. `ensure_cards_job()` registers a daily report job (idempotent — finds
-   existing job by reportTypeId before creating).
-2. `fetch_new_card_reports()` lists reports created since `since_iso`,
-   downloads each CSV, sums per-card numbers up to channel-day level, and
-   returns a list of (date, card_impressions, card_clicks, card_click_rate)
-   tuples.
-3. `upsert_card_metrics()` writes those daily rows into
-   `channel_metric_snapshots` with grain='daily' and metric_keys
-   `cardImpressions` / `cardClickRate` so the existing weekly aggregator
-   picks them up naturally.
-
-Reports lag: a freshly-created Reporting API job produces its first CSV
-~24-48h after creation; backfill (~30 days) follows over the next several
-days. Until the first report lands, the dashboard falls back to the
-Analytics API values that ingest/jobs.py still pulls.
-"""
-
-from __future__ import annotations
-
-import csv
-import io
-import logging
-from dataclasses import dataclass
-from pathlib import Path
-from typing import Iterable
-
-from googleapiclient.discovery import build
-
-from ingest.youtube_client import YouTubeClient
-
-_LOG = logging.getLogger(__name__)
-
-CARDS_REPORT_TYPE = "channel_cards_a1"
-JOB_NAME = "kpi-dashboard-cards"
-
-
-def _reporting_service(yt: YouTubeClient | None = None):
-    yt = yt or YouTubeClient()
-    return build("youtubereporting", "v1", credentials=yt._google_creds)
-
-
-def ensure_cards_job(svc=None) -> str:
-    """Return the job id for our cards report — create one if missing."""
-    svc = svc or _reporting_service()
-    existing = svc.jobs().list().execute().get("jobs", [])
-    for j in existing:
-        if j.get("reportTypeId") == CARDS_REPORT_TYPE:
-            return j["id"]
-    created = svc.jobs().create(
-        body={"reportTypeId": CARDS_REPORT_TYPE, "name": JOB_NAME}
-    ).execute()
-    _LOG.info("reporting: created job %s", created["id"])
-    return created["id"]
-
-
-@dataclass(frozen=True)
-class CardDailyRow:
-    date_iso: str       # YYYY-MM-DD
-    impressions: int
-    clicks: int
-    click_rate: float   # 0.0-1.0
-
-
-def _parse_yyyymmdd(s: str) -> str:
-    if len(s) == 8 and s.isdigit():
-        return f"{s[0:4]}-{s[4:6]}-{s[6:8]}"
-    return s
-
-
-def _aggregate_csv(csv_text: str) -> list[CardDailyRow]:
-    """Sum per-card rows up to channel-day level."""
-    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(csv_text))
-    by_day: dict[str, dict[str, float]] = {}
-    for row in reader:
-        d = _parse_yyyymmdd(row.get("date", ""))
-        if not d:
-            continue
-        bucket = by_day.setdefault(d, {"impressions": 0, "clicks": 0})
-        bucket["impressions"] += int(row.get("card_impressions") or 0)
-        bucket["clicks"] += int(row.get("card_clicks") or 0)
-    out: list[CardDailyRow] = []
-    for d, agg in sorted(by_day.items()):
-        impr = int(agg["impressions"])
-        clicks = int(agg["clicks"])
-        ctr = (clicks / impr) if impr else 0.0
-        out.append(CardDailyRow(date_iso=d, impressions=impr, clicks=clicks, click_rate=ctr))
-    return out
-
-
-def fetch_new_card_reports(
-    job_id: str,
-    *,
-    svc=None,
-    since_iso: str | None = None,
-) -> list[CardDailyRow]:
-    """List + download all reports newer than `since_iso`, return parsed rows.
-
-    The Reporting API REISSUES reports for the same `(startTime, endTime)`
-    window — for example, when YouTube backfills retroactive corrections.
-    Without deduping, a corrected reissue gets *added on top of* the original,
-    inflating every metric. Codex review 2026-04-26 [HIGH]: dedupe by window,
-    keep the newest `createTime` only.
-    """
-    svc = svc or _reporting_service()
-    kwargs: dict = {"jobId": job_id, "pageSize": 100}
-    if since_iso:
-        kwargs["createdAfter"] = since_iso
-    reports = svc.jobs().reports().list(**kwargs).execute().get("reports", [])
-    if not reports:
-        return []
-
-    # Dedupe per (startTime, endTime) — keep newest createTime.
-    by_window: dict[tuple[str, str], dict] = {}
-    for rep in reports:
-        key = (rep.get("startTime", ""), rep.get("endTime", ""))
-        prev = by_window.get(key)
-        if prev is None or rep.get("createTime", "") > prev.get("createTime", ""):
-            by_window[key] = rep
-
-    import requests
-    from google.auth.transport.requests import Request as _AuthReq
-
-    yt_creds = svc._http.credentials
-    if not yt_creds.valid:
-        yt_creds.refresh(_AuthReq())
-
-    out: list[CardDailyRow] = []
-    for rep in by_window.values():
-        url = rep.get("downloadUrl")
-        if not url:
-            continue
-        resp = requests.get(url, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {yt_creds.token}"}, timeout=60)
-        resp.raise_for_status()
-        out.extend(_aggregate_csv(resp.text))
-    return out
-
-
-def _iso_week_window(date_iso: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
-    """Map YYYY-MM-DD → (Monday, Sunday) of its ISO week."""
-    from datetime import date, timedelta
-
-    d = date.fromisoformat(date_iso)
-    monday = d - timedelta(days=d.isoweekday() - 1)
-    sunday = monday + timedelta(days=6)
-    return monday.isoformat(), sunday.isoformat()
-
-
-def upsert_card_metrics(
-    rows: Iterable[CardDailyRow],
-    *,
-    run_id: str,
-    db_path: str | Path | None = None,
-) -> int:
-    """Write Reporting-API card metrics into channel_metric_snapshots.
-
-    Two grains are written so the dashboard surfaces them immediately:
-      - daily   : raw per-day values from the CSV.
-      - weekly  : ISO-week aggregates (sum impressions, sum clicks, recompute
-                  CTR = clicks/impressions). Same metric_key as the existing
-                  Analytics-API path, so the latest-observed query picks the
-                  Reporting value because it lands later.
-    """
-    import sqlite3
-    from datetime import datetime, timezone
-
-    from app.db import db_path as default_db_path
-
-    path = str(db_path or default_db_path())
-    observed = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%fZ")
-    rows_list = list(rows)
-    written = 0
-
-    # Per-week aggregation for weekly-grain rows.
-    weekly: dict[tuple[str, str], dict[str, int]] = {}
-    for r in rows_list:
-        wk = _iso_week_window(r.date_iso)
-        bucket = weekly.setdefault(wk, {"impressions": 0, "clicks": 0})
-        bucket["impressions"] += r.impressions
-        bucket["clicks"] += r.clicks
-
-    with sqlite3.connect(path, timeout=10.0) as conn:
-        # Codex review r1 [LOW] + r3 follow-up: keep Python's connect-time
-        # 10s lock-wait and align PRAGMA busy_timeout to the same 10000ms
-        # (was 5000 in r1 patch, which actually LOWERED the effective wait;
-        # Python's `timeout=` arg already feeds busy_timeout internally).
-        conn.execute("PRAGMA busy_timeout=10000")
-
-        # daily rows
-        for r in rows_list:
-            for key, value in (
-                ("cardImpressions", float(r.impressions)),
-                ("cardClickRate", float(r.click_rate)),
-            ):
-                conn.execute(
-                    """
-                    INSERT OR REPLACE INTO channel_metric_snapshots
-                        (metric_key, grain, window_start, window_end,
-                         observed_on, value_num, run_id, preliminary)
-                    VALUES (?, 'daily', ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 0)
-                    """,
-                    (key, r.date_iso, r.date_iso, observed, value, run_id),
-                )
-                written += 1
-
-        # Codex review 2026-04-26 [HIGH]: weekly aggregates must come from the
-        # canonical day-level state in DB, not from the current `rows_list`
-        # batch. Otherwise a single late-arriving day (typical for Reporting
-        # API CSVs) would overwrite the previously-complete weekly snapshot
-        # with a partial-week total. Re-aggregate per ISO week from DB.
-        for (start, end) in weekly.keys():
-            agg_row = conn.execute(
-                """
-                WITH latest AS (
-                    SELECT metric_key, window_start, value_num,
-                           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
-                             PARTITION BY metric_key, window_start
-                             ORDER BY julianday(observed_on) DESC
-                           ) AS rn
-                    FROM channel_metric_snapshots
-                    WHERE grain = 'daily'
-                      AND metric_key IN ('cardImpressions', 'cardClickRate')
-                      AND window_start >= ? AND window_end <= ?
-                )
-                SELECT metric_key, SUM(value_num) AS total
-                FROM latest
-                WHERE rn = 1
-                GROUP BY metric_key
-                """,
-                (start, end),
-            ).fetchall()
-            agg_map = {row[0]: row[1] or 0.0 for row in agg_row}
-            impr = float(agg_map.get("cardImpressions", 0.0))
-            # cardClickRate is a daily ratio — to produce a weekly CTR we need
-            # the click count, which equals (cardImpressions × cardClickRate)
-            # per day summed. Recompute via a second pass to keep the SQL
-            # simple and explicit.
-            clicks_row = conn.execute(
-                """
-                WITH latest_pairs AS (
-                    SELECT window_start, metric_key, value_num,
-                           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
-                             PARTITION BY metric_key, window_start
-                             ORDER BY julianday(observed_on) DESC
-                           ) AS rn
-                    FROM channel_metric_snapshots
-                    WHERE grain = 'daily'
-                      AND metric_key IN ('cardImpressions', 'cardClickRate')
-                      AND window_start >= ? AND window_end <= ?
-                )
-                SELECT
-                    SUM(CASE WHEN metric_key='cardImpressions' THEN value_num ELSE 0 END
-                        * COALESCE((SELECT lp2.value_num FROM latest_pairs lp2
-                                    WHERE lp2.metric_key='cardClickRate'
-                                      AND lp2.window_start = latest_pairs.window_start
-                                      AND lp2.rn = 1), 0)) AS clicks
-                FROM latest_pairs
-                WHERE rn = 1 AND metric_key='cardImpressions'
-                """,
-                (start, end),
-            ).fetchone()
-            clicks = float((clicks_row[0] if clicks_row else 0) or 0)
-            ctr = (clicks / impr) if impr else 0.0
-
-            for key, value in (
-                ("cardImpressions", impr),
-                ("cardClickRate", ctr),
-            ):
-                conn.execute(
-                    """
-                    INSERT OR REPLACE INTO channel_metric_snapshots
-                        (metric_key, grain, window_start, window_end,
-                         observed_on, value_num, run_id, preliminary)
-                    VALUES (?, 'weekly', ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 0)
-                    """,
-                    (key, start, end, observed, value, run_id),
-                )
-                written += 1
-    return written
